items.title: Tour de France Braces for Chaos as Fans Flood Alpe d’Huez for Historic Double
items.author: ahood
items.content: An estimated 500,000 spectators pack Alpe d'Huez as Tour de France riders fear rowdy roadside crowds could derail the historic back-to-back summit weekend.

items.title: Sickness Crisis Threatens Tadej Pogačar and His Dream Tour de France Finale
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Team UAE torn apart by illness and injury as Pogačar looks to seal record-equalling fifth Tour de France yellow jersey in grueling Alpe d'Huez double.

items.title: Evenepoel Poised for Gripping Tour Showdown as Pogačar’s Team Fades
items.author: sstokes
items.content: Remco Evenepoel says he won’t take big risks heading into Alpe d’Huez, yet opportunities may arise with UAE under pressure.

items.title: Tour de France Stage 19 Preview: First Strike in Alpe d’Huez Double-Header
items.author: ahood
items.content: Tadej Pogačar is just days from a record-tying fifth Tour de France, but Friday serves up the first of two back-to-back summit finishes atop Alpe d’Huez.

items.title: Tour de France Stage 18: Carapaz Gives EF Education-Easypost A Huge Tour Stage Win
items.author: sstokes
items.content: EF Education EasyPost rider Richard Carapaz strongest on the final climb to solo to victory, Matteo Jorgenson third.

items.title: Tadej Pogačar Short-Handed for Alpe d’Huez After Brandon McNulty Exit
items.author: ahood
items.content: Tadej Pogačar is closing in on a fifth Tour de France title, but UAE suddenly enters the decisive final weekend in the Alps battered by illness and attrition.

items.title: Tour de France Rider Knocked off Bike by Driver Who Then Blamed Him for It on Instagram
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Xandro Meurisse has insult added to injury after being hit by driver in second apparent road rage case in one day at the Tour de France.

items.title: No One Can Stop Tadej Pogačar at the Tour de France. Here Are 5 Radical Ways to Even the Odds
items.author: ahood
items.content: The Slovenian is closing in on his fifth Tour de France. Here are some serious and downright wacky ideas to give his rivals a chance next July.

items.title: Weddings, Taxis and Video Games: The Tour Peloton’s Funniest Drug Test Stories
items.author: Andy McGrath
items.content: Being available to be tested every day of the year throws up strange and amusing scenarios. Kuss, O'Connor and other stars tell all.

items.title: Tadej Pogačar Is Already Plotting His Ultimate Tour de France Coronation on Alpe d’Huez
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Preview: Marquee records and iconic victories in play as back-to-back Alpe stages crown the hardest final of the Tour de France in a decade.

items.title: The UCI Just Fined a Tour de France Rider for Peeing in Public. It Was Inevitable.
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: After bottle controversy at the Giro d'Italia, it took officials 17 stages to catch a rider making an untimely toilet stop at the Tour de France.

items.title: Tour de France Stage 18 Preview: The First of a Three-Stage Climbing Finale
items.author: Will Tracy
items.content: Stage 18 opens three days in the mountains to close out the 2026 Tour.

items.title: ‘This Is Effectively a Warrant’: Sports Scientist Speaks on the Tour de France Night Time Doping Tests
items.author: sstokes
items.content: Sports scientist Ross Tucker discusses the controversial decision to test Tour de France riders at night.

items.title: Tour de France Stage 17: Jasper Philipsen Reignites Green Jersey Race with Breakaway Win
items.author: ahood
items.content: Tour de France Stage 17 results: Belgian star rescued his Tour with a breakaway victory featuring Quinn Simmons as UAE mountain lieutenant Adam Yates suffered before three decisive Alpine stages.

items.title: One Second, Three Titles, and Glitter on the Podium: FoCo Fondo Year 11
items.author: Kelly Matkovich
items.content: A heartbreaking disqualification handed Caleb Bottcher the men's sprint by the thinnest of margins. Lauren Stephens made it three straight. Robin Cummings stopped to reapply glitter mid-race and still won by an hour.

items.title: The Amount of Gear in a Tour de France Team Truck Is Staggering
items.author: Dave Everett
items.content: It takes a lot more than just bikes to survive the Tour. Here is a look at the exact inventory Lotto Dstny packs to fuel the riders and fix the fleet.

items.title: Garmin Buys TrainingPeaks in Fitness Landscape Power Move
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Garmin acquires TrainingPeaks and TrainHeroic to bring two leading training and data platforms into its tech empire.

items.title: VIDEO: Tadej Pogačar Somehow Stays Upright and Avoids Tour de France Disaster
items.author: ahood
items.content: Roadside video shows Pogačar using incredible bike-handling skills to escape crashing in the same high-speed corner that ended Florian Lipowitz’s Tour.

items.title: Every Tour de France Rider Tested in Rest Day Anti-Doping Sweep
items.author: ahood
items.content: The ITA collected samples from the entire Tour peloton for biological passport analysis amid criticism over surprise overnight controls as UCI shows public support.

items.title: 1 Ban, $10k Fines, GC Penalties: UCI Hits Sanction Overdrive at the Tour de France
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Lidl-Trek DS whacked with huge fine and Tom Pidcock docked time in the two most sanctioned stages of the Tour on Sunday and Tuesday.

items.title: Attack or Bust: Matteo Jorgenson Is Determined to Take His Big Chance
items.author: Andy McGrath
items.content: US star approaches Alpine stages with 'glass half full' and reflects on Vingegaard's last supper, complete with McDonald's order.

items.title: What You Need to Know About the Next-Gen Fuel Powering Pogačar at the Tour de France
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Tadej Pogačar is using a wave of first-ever lactate gels and drinks that could transform pro cycling. But their full performance impact remains a mystery.

items.title: Tour de France Stage 17 Preview: The Last Chance Saloon on a Classic Transition Stage
items.author: Will Tracy
items.content: Opportunities are dwindling at the Tour in the final week, especially for non-climbers.

items.title: Florian Lipowitz Out of 2026 Tour de France After High-Speed Crash on Stage 16 Time Trial
items.author: ahood
items.content: Red Bull team feels 'mixed emotions' at bittersweet moment after German crashes out of the race on a right-hand corner, leaving teammate Evenepoel without a key helper for the final week.

items.title: Tour de France Stage 16: Evenepoel Obliterates Time Trial After Teammate Lipowitz Crashes Out
items.author: Jim Cotton
items.content: Highs and lows for Red Bull: GC hopeful Lipowitz abandons after high-speed crash as Pogačar places second to Evenepoel's TT masterclass.
